# Transcrição: 02. LinkedIn

- **Fonte:** 02. LinkedIn
- **Duração:** 04:59
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey guys, Brandon Smiths here, super excited to bring you this module on LinkedIn prospecting. So a little bit about my background, I've had the very good fortune to work with a lot of different companies on implementing lead generation and prospecting from LinkedIn. And so I have a lot of experience on what works, what doesn't, and in general, a really solid method of going about generating new business using LinkedIn as the catalyst, as the channel. Now I know I said lead generation a little bit ago, but something that's going to be really important, important enough for me to address in the beginning is the way that I look at LinkedIn prospecting is that you have to look at it as a relationship based pursuit. Here's what I mean. If you plan to go on LinkedIn and spam everybody, asking them for a call, telling them how awesome you are and you know, throwing them a link, even though your intentions may be very good, for a lot of people that's going to be brusque, pretty rude, and I'm sure you've had this experience being on the other end of that, it's not going to be all that effective, it will actually work. Look at me wrong, but you're going to alienate many more prospects than you would if you took a much more relational approach. And what I found is that oftentimes seeds that I plant on LinkedIn come back a long time later to talk to me and I've closed a considerable amount of business from people who I nurture it and talk to for a while beforehand. So I'm going to share with you the system that I use for myself and for my clients to generate new business on LinkedIn. I'm going to now overview what that system looks like. So it is a three pillar system. The first pillar is all about your profile. How can you optimize your LinkedIn profile to generate opportunities on LinkedIn? The second pillar is finding your potential prospects on LinkedIn, right? Searching for prospects, how do you find them? And then we'll also be connecting with these prospects at that point. So as you can see, the profile ties very nicely into connecting with people, right? If your profile is attractive to the pain of your prospect, they're much more likely to connect with you. You will see much more relevant to them. You won't necessarily look like some stranger who's connecting with them. Ideally, you're looking like an expert in something that they just so happen to be trying

## [03:02 - 04:59]

to solve in their business. And then the third and final and most important and difficult step. How do you nurture the new connections that you get from searching for prospects and move them from a LinkedIn connection into becoming a lead and unlocking a business opportunity with them? So we'll be going over all of that in the next modules. I do want to mention one other element that will massively help you in this effort, which is if you're able to post content on LinkedIn with some regularity, your efforts here will be much more effective. Your prospects who are active on LinkedIn will see you on there posting organically. Ideally they may be commenting. And perhaps the most high value thing that you can do is to actually engage with them on posts that they make inside of the platform if they're doing so. All that being said, I'm super excited to bring you this training. Again, I want to encourage you to look at this as not a lead generation process, but a relationship generation process. One of the things that commonly has happened to me in campaigns that I've run for myself and my clients is that people will say, hey, I don't actually need what you're really good at, but boy, do I know someone who does. And trust me, you will not get that if you are perceived as being pushy, if you are perceived as being salesy. So these are the two main things that we're trying to avoid. All right, I'm really excited to share this stuff with you in the next module about profiles.
